name: General Tso Chicken Variant (Jerry's)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into 1/2 inch chunks.
2. Combine the dry sherry, soy sauce, and cornstarch in a bowl.
3. Marinate the chicken in the mixture for 30 minutes.
4. Cook some rice to serve alongside this dish.
5. In a separate bowl, combine the chicken broth, rice vinegar, remaining soy sauce, sesame oil, and sugar to create the sauce.
6. Cut the vegetables (onion, red or green bell pepper, broccoli, and mushroom) into 1/2 inch chunks.
7. Mince the ginger and garlic.
8. Heat a wok over high heat. Add peanut oil (and any other oil if desired).
9. Add the chicken to the wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes until lightly browned.
10. Add minced garlic, ginger, onion, and red pepper flakes. Stir-fry for another minute.
11. Pour in the sauce and add broccoli. Stir-fry for 1 minute until the vegetables are just cooked through.
12. Add the bell pepper and mushrooms. Stir-fry for 1 minute until they are slightly softened.
13. Mix together a cornstarch slurry (cornstarch with water) and stir it into the wok.
14. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and the chicken is nicely glazed.
15. Add more cornstarch mixture if needed to achieve desired consistency.
16. Turn off the heat and serve over cooked rice.
